task_description: Find a nearby hotel for a short stay near the Tea Ware Museum, narrow results by budget, and open the Imperial Hotel listing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows In the zoomed-in crop, the semitransparent red X is centered on the middle round action button labeled “附近,” which has a location-pin style icon inside a light blue circle. It is one of the place action controls beneath the tabs in the Google Maps place panel. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to Google Maps showing the place “茶具文物馆,” with the left information panel visible and the map on the right. The X is on a UI control in the place action row, between “保存” and “发送到手机.”
caption.think: The user likely wants to use the Nearby feature for this museum location, probably to explore places near the selected site.
caption.action: Click the “附近” nearby button in the place action row.
caption.expectation: Google Maps should activate the nearby search for this place, updating the panel or search results to show places near 茶具文物馆.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a Google Maps nearby-results dropdown list beneath the search bar. The semitransparent red X is centered on the second list item labeled “酒店,” with the smaller subtitle “附近的” underneath. The item has a black bed icon on its left, identifying it as the Hotels category. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to Google Maps for “茶具文物馆,” with the left place-information panel still visible and a small nearby category suggestion list expanded near the top-left over the map. This is a UI control in the search suggestion/category list, not a map object.
caption.think: After opening Nearby for the museum, the user likely wants to search for hotels near this location by selecting the Hotels category from the nearby suggestions.
caption.action: Click the “酒店” item with the bed icon in the nearby suggestions list.
caption.expectation: Google Maps should select that category and update the nearby search to show hotels near 茶具文物馆, refreshing the list and/or map markers.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the hotel search filters area in Google Maps. A semitransparent red X is centered on the left date field displaying “4月24日周五,” immediately to the right of a small calendar icon and to the left of the second date field “4月25日周六.” This target is a UI control in the top filter bar, specifically the check-in date selector, not a map object. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open to Google Maps with the query “酒店,” a left results panel listing hotels and a large map on the right covered with hotel price markers around Hong Kong.
caption.think: After selecting hotels nearby, the user likely wants to adjust the stay dates, starting with the check-in date, to refine hotel availability and pricing.
caption.action: Click the left date field labeled “4月24日周五” next to the calendar icon.
caption.expectation: The check-in date selector should become active, likely opening a date-picker calendar or highlighting the field for date selection.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows an open date-picker calendar. A semitransparent red X is centered on the day number “27” in the calendar grid, indicating a clickable date cell within the popup calendar. The surrounding dates include “20, 21, 22, 23” above and “28, 29, 30” to the right, confirming it is a specific day button rather than blank space. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to Google Maps hotel results in Hong Kong. The left panel shows the hotel search filters with the check-in and check-out date fields, and the calendar popup is expanded under the date field. This target is a UI control in the date-picker popup.
caption.think: The user has opened the hotel date selector and is now choosing a specific check-in date, likely changing the stay from the previously shown date to the 27th to update hotel availability and prices.
caption.action: Click the date cell labeled “27” in the calendar popup.
caption.expectation: The date “27” should become selected as the active date, and the date field should update immediately, with the calendar either remaining open for the next date selection or advancing to the next step in the date range selection.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows an open Google Maps date-picker calendar for “2026年4月.” A semitransparent red X is centered on the date cell labeled “28,” immediately to the right of the already selected teal-filled “27” date. This indicates a click on a specific calendar day button, which is a UI control rather than the map itself. In the full-screen view, Chrome is displaying Google Maps hotel results in Hong Kong, with the left search/results panel visible and the calendar popup expanded beneath the stay date fields.
caption.think: The user has already chosen the 27th and is now selecting the next day, likely setting the check-out date to April 28 to define a one-night hotel stay and update results accordingly.
caption.action: Click the date cell labeled “28” in the calendar popup.
caption.expectation: The 28th should become selected as the second date in the range, the stay dates should update to April 27–28, and the calendar may close while the hotel results refresh for the new date range.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red drag path running horizontally from left to right, ending on a teal circular slider handle. This indicates a drag on a range slider control, specifically moving the handle leftward from its current far-right position. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to Google Maps hotel results in Hong Kong. The left panel contains a price filter slider labeled “$0 - $2,700+,” and the teal handle on the right end of that slider matches the crop, confirming this is the maximum-price handle for narrowing hotel results by price.
caption.think: After changing the stay dates, the user is likely refining hotel search results by lowering the maximum allowed price using the right handle of the price range slider.
caption.action: Click-and-hold on the right teal circular handle of the price slider labeled “$0 - $2,700+,” drag along the shown path to the left to reduce the maximum price, then release.
caption.expectation: The maximum-price limit should decrease, the displayed price range label should update, and the hotel results and map markers should refresh to show only hotels within the new budget.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered over the hotel result card area beside the thumbnail image and price badge, directly on the listing text for “帝国酒店,” around the rating/review count line “(1,207).” This is a clickable hotel result item in the left results panel, not a toolbar control, blank area, or handle. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to Google Maps hotel results in Hong Kong, with the price filter already reduced and the left sidebar listing hotels. The targeted item is the first visible result card for “帝国酒店,” which also shows amenity icons like Wi‑Fi and dining and a green price badge on the thumbnail.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the selected hotel’s details after filtering by dates and price, to inspect this specific result more closely on Google Maps.
caption.action: Click the hotel result card for “帝国酒店” in the left results panel.
caption.expectation: That hotel listing should become selected, and Google Maps should open the hotel’s detail panel or place page, likely highlighting its location on the map.
